title: How to Start a Gelato Business: Complete Equipment & Cost Guide for 2026
author: BEJADY Team
excerpt: A step-by-step guide to starting your gelato business in 2026: equipment costs, machine selection, store setup, and OEM partnership options.

children.text: Why 2026 Is the Perfect Year to Start a Gelato Business

The global gelato market is projected to reach $36.2 billion by 2033, growing at 5.2% CAGR (Grand View Research, 2024). With rising consumer demand for premium, authentic frozen desserts, there has never been a better time to enter the gelato industry. Whether you're planning a small artisanal shop or a chain concept, the key to success starts with choosing the right equipment.
Step 1: Choose Your Gelato Machine

The most important decision you'll make is selecting the right batch freezer. Gelato machines operate differently from soft-serve or industrial ice cream freezers. They use a batch freezing process where a fixed amount of liquid mix is frozen while being gently agitated, typically achieving 20-30% overrun — much lower than American ice cream's 50-80%, resulting in a denser, more flavorful product.

BEJADY offers five models to match every business scale, from the single-cylinder machine producing 10-14 L/h for cafes and startups to the six-cylinder flagship model delivering 60-80 L/h for high-volume chains and industrial production.
Single Cylinder (10-14 L/h) — Ideal for cafes, bakeries, and small startups. Compact countertop design, 1.5 kW power, 80 kg weight.
Double Cylinder (20-28 L/h) — Dual flavor simultaneous production. Touch screen control, automatic wash cycle, 2.8 kW.
Triple Cylinder (30-40 L/h) — Three flavors at once. Recipe memory storage, pre-cooling function, 4.2 kW.
Four Cylinder (40-55 L/h) — High-volume production. Rapid cooling system, heavy-duty build, 5.6 kW.
Six Cylinder (60-80 L/h) — Industrial flagship. Programmable scheduling, remote diagnostics, 8.4 kW.
Step 2: Budget Your Equipment Investment

One of the biggest advantages of partnering with a Chinese OEM manufacturer like BEJADY is cost efficiency. While Italian-branded machines can cost $14,000+, BEJADY offers identical quality at 1/5 to 1/10 the price — starting from under $3,000. This price gap is not about quality difference; it's about manufacturing efficiency and supply chain optimization.
Your total startup equipment budget should include:
Gelato batch freezer: $2,800 - $8,000 (BEJADY range based on cylinder count)
Display freezer (for serving): $2,000 - $5,000
Storage freezer / hardening cabinet: $1,500 - $4,000
Pasteurizer (if making base from scratch): $3,000 - $6,000
Store fixtures and point-of-sale: $3,000 - $8,000
Step 3: OEM Partnership — The Smart Way to Launch

For many entrepreneurs, an OEM/ODM partnership with an established manufacturer is the fastest path to market. BEJADY's 20 years of export experience means we handle everything from machine customization (branding, voltage, compressor selection) to recipe development and after-sales support. Our proprietary AI formula database covers 35+ gelato recipes across multiple base types, so you can launch with a proven product lineup.

The OEM process is straightforward: inquiry and consultation → design and quotation → prototyping and approval → mass production → QC and packaging → global delivery. Typical lead time is 25-35 days, with faster options available for urgent orders.
Step 4: Location and Store Setup
A successful gelato shop typically needs 300-800 sq ft of space. Key considerations:
High foot traffic areas near shopping districts or tourist spots perform best
Gelato display case should be visible from the street to attract walk-in customers
Behind-the-counter workspace needs adequate ventilation and drainage
Storage area for dry ingredients and cold storage for dairy
Step 5: Recipe Development and Training

Gelato is as much about science as it is about art. The key parameters that define gelato quality — fat content (4-8%), sugar content (15-24%), serving temperature (-10 to -12°C), and overrun (20-30%) — all depend on precise formulation and machine settings. BEJADY provides comprehensive training covering machine operation, maintenance, and gelato-making techniques, plus dedicated recipe development for OEM partners.
